title: Two questions about the Angels
wikitext:
[]: Two questions about the Angels.             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Sep 17, 2009 1:58 pm First, why did they get the names that they did? Second, what did they do with the leftover corpses?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Sep 17, 2009 2:28 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=514

[]:             Quote Post by UltraBlackPilot » Fri Sep 18, 2009 9:43 pm All there names mean something similar to the angels of God I.E. Zeruel = Angel of strength There corpses where probably studied afterwards.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sun Sep 20, 2009 6:41 am The names are chosen from Judeo-Christian angelic mythology to fit the physical and probably psychological characteristics of each Angel. Shamshel, for example, was named after the angel standing watch over the Garden of Eden with two flaming swords going back and forth in front of it, a reference to the anime Angel's energy whips. Take also for example Leliel, which means Jaws of God and Ramiel meaning Thunder of God .

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Sep 20, 2009 9:24 am On a related note, all the angels follow a similar naming scheme, in that they translate to 'X of God' , with 'El' used to denote God, save Tabris who uses a derivative of 'Jah'... However Sandalphon seemingly goes against this convention altogether...his name roughly translates to co-brother (in a similar sense to co-worker)...or possibly 'one who wears sandals' but seems unlikely... He is described as being a brother figure to Metatron (and at times, mistaken for the same entity)...and is said to wage eternal battle together with Michael against Sammael... Personally, I think his breaking the naming scheme in Eva was solely because they wanted an Angel associated with embryos (As Sandalphon is said to determine an embryo's gender)

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Sep 20, 2009 9:58 am Hey Teague, does IrEUl break teh scheme too, or are the consonants the only things that really matter in this particular Hebtrew-y word?

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Sep 20, 2009 7:28 pm It's Iru-EL...although I have seen it spelled the other way...

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Sep 20, 2009 7:35 pm Nope, it's Ireul. (Check the wiki) But I did some research into Hebrew and answered my own question. Iruel is short for Yereuel, which has the, you know, "el" symbol. So my comment is retracted.
